Details: Removal of uranium from water by ion exchange can be very effective (greater than 99 percent removal in most cases). Ion exchange is a Best Available Technology for the control of uranium. It is dependent upon the resin type, regeneration frequency, competing ions, and initial concentration. The most common resin used was an anionic resin (Dowex or Purolite). Limited regeneration studies were available, but based on the limited data, uranium removal appears to be unaffected by multiple regenerations.  Most studies did not differentiate between which form of uranium was being removed.
